title: World of Warcraft App
description: A mobile companion for World of Warcraft, designed from concept to production used by millions of users... later retired due to botting issues
detailIntro: Designing from scratch a high-quality mobile companion app for the World of Warcraft universe

detailSections.title: Context
copy: World of Warcraft has a deep and highly detailed player experience, with layers of characters, progression systems, guilds, quests, items, achievements, and community interaction. The challenge was to design a companion app that could simplify this complexity without stripping away the atmosphere and depth that players expect from the franchise. The product needed to feel useful, immersive, and credible to a gaming audience while also being clear enough to present as a commercially valuable mobile product.

The app was created within an investor-led environment, meaning the design had to do more than look good. It needed to demonstrate product potential, show how the experience could fit into the wider World of Warcraft ecosystem, and communicate a compelling vision that could be understood by both business stakeholders and gaming users.

detailSections.title: Approach
copy: My approach focused on combining strong mobile UX principles with high-quality visual design suited to a fantasy gaming product. I explored how core companion app features could be organised into simple, accessible journeys, allowing users to quickly access character information, progression updates, social features, and in-game related content without feeling overwhelmed.

I created interface designs that balanced usability with atmosphere, using clear layouts, strong visual hierarchy, game-inspired styling, and intuitive interaction patterns. The goal was to make the app feel premium and familiar to players while still functioning like a modern mobile product. I also considered how the design could support investor conversations by making the product vision easy to understand, visually impressive, and commercially compelling.

detailSections.title: Outcome
copy: The final designs helped position the companion app as a polished, high-value mobile product concept capable of extending the World of Warcraft experience beyond the main game. The UI work gave the product a strong visual identity, clear user journeys, and a professional level of presentation that supported stakeholder and investor confidence. The project demonstrated my ability to translate a complex entertainment IP into a refined mobile experience, balancing fan expectations, product usability, and commercial storytelling.

title: Vodafone new features
description: Worked with a large global team of designers, expanding the product offering including gamification, using the same components in a more innovative way to attract and keep customers.
detailIntro: Vodafone wanted to add gamification and inspiration from social media apps to retain users

media.caption: Vodafone full page ss
media.alt: Major Telecoms brand product updates media

detailSections.title: Context
copy: Vodafone’s mobile apps served a wide and diverse user base across different regions, each with varying needs, behaviours, and expectations. The challenge was not only to modernise the app experience, but to make it more engaging and valuable enough for users to return to regularly. Traditional telecom app experiences can often feel purely transactional, focused on checking usage, managing bills, or accessing support. Vodafone wanted to explore ways to make the experience feel more dynamic, personalised, and rewarding while still maintaining clarity, trust, and brand consistency at scale.

detailSections.title: Approach
copy: My approach focused on combining strong product thinking with user-centred design. I explored how familiar interaction patterns from social media apps could be adapted into a telecom environment, making the experience feel more intuitive, engaging, and repeatable. This included concepts around gamification, rewards, progress, personalised content, and more interactive journeys designed to encourage users to engage with the app beyond basic account management.

I worked closely with Vodafone stakeholders from different regions, running and contributing to meetings that helped align product goals, market needs, business priorities, and design direction. Through design exploration, wireframing, prototyping, stakeholder feedback, and iteration, I helped shape app experiences that could work across multiple markets while still feeling relevant to local users. The work required balancing creativity with enterprise-level constraints, ensuring that new design ideas could sit comfortably within Vodafone’s wider ecosystem.

detailSections.title: Outcome
copy: The work helped Vodafone explore a more engaging direction for their app experiences, moving beyond purely functional journeys toward a more interactive, retention-focused product experience. By introducing gamification principles and social media-inspired interaction patterns, the designs created opportunities to increase repeat engagement, strengthen customer loyalty, and make the app feel more useful in users’ daily lives. The project also helped align global stakeholders around a clearer product vision, supporting a scalable design direction that could be adapted across Vodafone markets.

title: NHS project built on salesforce
description: A comprehensive redesign of an in-depth web based research platform tailored to medical researchers. It was just as complex as it sounds!
detailIntro: Redesigning the Health Data Gateway from scratch.

detailSections.title: Context
copy: The Health Data Gateway had grown into a powerful platform containing thousands of datasets, studies, and research resources. However, users faced challenges navigating large volumes of information, understanding dataset relationships, and finding relevant resources efficiently. The platform also needed to accommodate the needs of diverse user groups, each with different goals, technical knowledge, and research requirements. The challenge was to transform a highly complex system into an intuitive experience that made health data more discoverable, accessible, and actionable.

detailSections.title: Approach
copy: I led UX activities focused on understanding user behaviours, pain points, and research workflows. Through stakeholder workshops, user research, journey mapping, and information architecture exercises, I identified opportunities to simplify navigation and improve discoverability. I restructured key areas of the platform, designed clearer search and filtering experiences, refined content hierarchies, and created scalable design patterns that could support future platform expansion. Throughout the process, I worked closely with researchers, subject matter experts, developers, and product stakeholders to ensure both user needs and technical requirements were addressed.

detailSections.title: Outcome
copy: The redesign delivered a more intuitive and user-centred experience for discovering and exploring health data resources. By simplifying complex user journeys and improving information architecture, users were able to navigate the platform more efficiently and access relevant datasets with greater confidence. The work established a scalable foundation for future product development while helping Health Data Research UK further its mission of enabling data-driven health research across the UK.

title: Ai Built Global Recipe Platform
description: Giving chefs worldwide a platform to monetize their recipes. I designed the complete flow and used Ai (Claude, Codex, Gemini) to create a fully functional SAAS with a managed backend (Convex) and onboarding customers real time
detailIntro: Locally designed and Ai developed SAAS platform

media.alt: Ai Built Global Recipe Platform media
Ai Built Global Recipe Platform media

detailSections.title: Context
copy: With the rise of user-generated content, but also the  difficulty in being able to find recipes from different cultures, there was clearly a gap. Actually going out and talking to chefs, especially from other countries, seeing their pain points in being able to access global markets and monetize their recipes despite their large following was how the idea was born. Especially with the success of Patreon and other user-based-content platforms.

detailSections.title: Approach
copy: I began with intensive research into competitors and existing products, documenting key insights and creating a comparative table to identify where each product excelled, where gaps existed, and where my concept could stand out. From there, I developed mockups around a clear USP, tested and refined the user flow, and gradually moved toward a finalised design.

The next stage was moving into an AI-powered IDE, starting with Cursor, to begin developing the product while using the completed designs as a reference point. This became a valuable learning project, giving me hands-on experience across frontend, backend, and the wider development cycle, ultimately helping us reach the intended goal.

detailSections.title: Outcome
copy: A SAAS that users can upload their recipes and share their profiles to their page where their followers can pay for their recipes and Stripe will pay them individually as well as pay the platform percentage.
